instruction:
Let the population (X) be uniformly distributed on ([0, \theta]). A sample of size 1, (X_1), is drawn from this population. Which of the following (\hat{\theta}) is an unbiased estimator of (\theta)?

Options:
A. (\hat{\theta} = \frac{5}{3}X_1)
B. (\hat{\theta} = \frac{3}{2}X_1)
C. (\hat{\theta} = X_1 +  rac{\theta}{2})
D. (\hat{\theta} = \frac{4}{3}X_1)
E. (\hat{\theta} = 2X_1)
F. (\hat{\theta} = \frac{6}{3}X_1)
G. (\hat{\theta} = \frac{7}{3}X_1)
H. (\hat{\theta} = \frac{5}{2}X_1)
I. (\hat{\theta} = X_1)
J. (\hat{\theta} =  rac{1}{2}X_1)
